item.title: About
item.description: Wilbur Watts M.Ed. Candidate, Mathematics Education · Penn State University Welcome to my portfolio. I'm Wilbur Watts — a Penn State master's student dedicated to making rigorous math education accessible to every learner, whether they're in a college classroom or a workforce training program. This space reflects my journey as both a practitioner and a scholar: the curricula I've designed, the pedagogical questions I'm exploring, and my ongoing commitment to meeting students exactly where ...

item.title: Specialization
item.description: Area of Specialization As a Mathematics Education student at Pennsylvania State University, my area of specialization focuses on preparing to teach secondary mathematics (grades 7–12) through equitable, research-based instructional practices. My coursework and field experiences emphasize deep conceptual understanding in algebra, geometry, calculus, and statistics, alongside the pedagogical content knowledge necessary to make these subjects meaningful and accessible to diverse learners. I am...

item.title: Competency
item.description: My professional expertise spans three interconnected domains: equity-centered mathematics curriculum design, post-secondary and workforce mathematics instruction, and the study of mathematical identity development among non-traditional learners. These areas reflect both the practical knowledge I have built through years of direct instruction and tutoring, and the scholarly grounding I have developed through graduate study at Penn State University. Areas of Expertise Equity-Centered Curriculum...
